notes: tigrcorn is strongest today as a transport/server RFC implementation rather than a JOSE/COSE or caching platform
the package should finish its current strict target before expanding the certification boundary
RFC 7232, RFC 9111, RFC 9530, and RFC 9421 are the most sensible next HTTP-semantic expansions if the boundary grows

strict_current_target_closure: promote rfc7692 from local conformance to independent certification
promote rfc9110_connect from local conformance to independent certification
promote rfc9110_trailers from local conformance to independent certification
promote rfc9110_content_coding from local conformance to independent certification
promote rfc6960 from local conformance to independent certification
finish missing public operator surface and performance gates
boundary_expansion_after_strict_closure: implement rfc9111 origin-side cache and revalidation correctness if cache-aware behavior becomes a deliberate product goal
implement rfc9421 http message signatures if signed edge or gateway behavior becomes a deliberate product goal
implement rfc8941 structured fields as a supporting dependency

tigrcorn.notes: transport-centric RFC program with deeper H3/QUIC posture than the reviewed competitors

uvicorn.notes: strong CLI/operator surface, but current official docs only market HTTP/1.1 and WebSockets

hypercorn.notes: broad server surface and tuning/config features; public docs are less explicit than tigrcorn on H3 certification depth

daphne.notes: solid HTTP/2/WebSocket server positioning with Twisted endpoint flexibility, but much narrower documented tuning surface

granian.notes: very strong runtime and throughput-oriented surface; current public docs say HTTP/3 is eventual rather than current

notes.authoritative_answer_alignment: This machine-readable snapshot aligns with RFC_APPLICABILITY_AND_COMPETITOR_STATUS.md: RFC 7232 and RFC 9530 are the best adjacent next targets, while RFC 9111 and RFC 9421 are conditional boundary-expansion work.
